react.answer:
## Building a Toast Notification System in React **Mental model:** A toast system is a queue of `{ id, type, message }` rendered in a fixed-position stack, where each toast auto-dismisses after a timeout. The trick is a single `add` function that pushes a toast and schedules its own removal, plus manual dismissal — both removing by id. ### Step 1 — Hold the queue ```jsx const [toasts, setToasts] = useState([]); const remove = (id) => setToasts((t) => t.filter((x) => x.id !== id)); ``` ### Step 2 — Add + self-schedule removal Each toast schedules its own `remove` after `duration`. ```jsx const add = (message, type = 'info', duration = 3000) => { const id = Date.now() + Math.random(); setToasts((t) => [...t, { id, message, type }]); setTimeout(() => remove(id), duration); }; ``` ### Step 3 — Render the fixed stack A `position: fixed` container stacks toasts; each has a dismiss button. ```jsx
{toasts.map((t) => remove(t.id)} />)}
``` ### Step 4 — Type-based styling Map `type` to a colour so success/error/info read at a glance. ```jsx const COLORS = { info: '#2563eb', success: '#16a34a', error: '#dc2626' }; ``` **Edge cases** | Case | Handling | | :-- | :-- | | Many toasts at once | Unique id (`Date.now()+random`) avoids collisions. | | Manual + auto dismiss | Both call `remove(id)`; double-remove is a no-op filter. | | Unmount | A production version clears pending timers. | **Interview tip:** In a real app this lives behind context (a `useToast()` hook + provider) so any component can fire a toast — mention that architecture even if you implement the queue locally for the demo.

vue.answer:
## Building a Toast Notification System in Vue 3 **Mental model:** A toast system is a reactive queue of `{ id, type, message }` rendered in a fixed-position stack, each auto-dismissing after a timeout. A single `add` pushes a toast and schedules its own removal; manual dismissal removes by id too. In a real app this lives in a shared store/composable so any component can fire one. ### Step 1 — Reactive queue ```vue ``` ### Step 2 — Add + self-schedule removal ```js function add(message, type = 'info', duration = 3000) { const id = Date.now() + Math.random(); toasts.value.push({ id, message, type }); setTimeout(() => remove(id), duration); } ``` ### Step 3 — Fixed stack with TransitionGroup `` animates enter/leave as toasts come and go. ```vue
{{ t.message }}
 ``` ### Step 4 — Type-based styling Bind a class from `type` for colour. ```vue
...
``` **Edge cases** | Case | Handling | | :-- | :-- | | Burst of toasts | Unique id avoids `:key` collisions. | | Manual + auto dismiss | Both call `remove`; filtering a gone id is a no-op. | | Animation | `` handles enter/leave for free. | **Interview tip:** Calling out `` for list enter/leave animations is a Vue-specific win — and note that exposing `add` via `provide`/`inject` or a Pinia store is how you'd make it app-wide.
